message:
fix(workers): reject unsafe worker IDs + shell-escape IDs in remote commands

Worker IDs flow from operator-supplied `workers.toml` into three distinct
interpolation contexts: (1) remote shell commands over SSH for telemetry
and hook installation; (2) local filesystem paths for caches, control
sockets, and reservations; (3) HTTP route segments in the daemon API
(`POST /workers/{id}/drain`). None of those contexts re-validate; they
just splice the ID in. A worker ID of `"; rm -rf /"` would turn an
operator-supplied config into arbitrary code execution on the worker
the next time the daemon polled telemetry.

Two complementary fixes:

1. rchd/src/reload.rs — gate the config at load time
   `validate_workers_config` now rejects any worker whose ID fails
   `is_safe_worker_id`. The allow-list is ASCII alphanumerics plus
   `_`, `-`, `.`; 1-64 chars; no leading dot (hidden-file confusion).
   `/` and `:` are explicitly disallowed even though they survive most
   shells — `/` would re-interpret cache paths, `:` would re-interpret
   HTTP route segments.

   Rejected set exercised by test: command separator (`;rm -rf /`),
   command substitution (`$(id)`, backticks), pipe (`|cat`), background
   (`&`), whitespace, newline, path separator, URL separator, quotes,
   backslash, empty, leading dot, over-length (65 chars). Each
   rejection panics the config load with an explicit error naming the
   offending ID and the allowed character set.

   Also (same file, adjacent code): corrects bookkeeping in
   `apply_worker_diff`. `result.removed` was incremented every loop
   iteration, including for workers that entered draining (with jobs
   in flight), which stayed in the pool until their jobs finished.
   Now only workers actually removed from the pool increment `removed`
   — drained-but-still-present workers surface via the existing
   warnings path. Prevents the reload summary from claiming workers
   were removed when they're still scheduling builds.

2. rchd/src/telemetry.rs — defense in depth at the call site
   `collect_telemetry_from_worker` now shell-escapes the worker ID
   via `shell_escape::escape` before splicing into the remote
   command template. Mirrors the hardening already done in
   rch/src/hook.rs for the same reason. Belt-and-braces: with
   (1) in place the escape is redundant today, but a future config
   loader that relaxed the allow-list (e.g. to accept `:` for
   URL-like IDs) would otherwise silently reopen the injection.

Tests:
  test_is_safe_worker_id_accepts_normal_names (canonical shapes)
  test_is_safe_worker_id_rejects_shell_metacharacters (15 bad cases)
  test_validate_workers_config_rejects_unsafe_id (integration)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
